text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful framework is setting SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. This ensures everyone understands exactly what needs to be done. Once you have your main goal, break it down into smaller milestones. These are key checkpoints that help you track progress and stay on schedule. Each milestone should lead to a deliverable, a tangible product or result, such as a report or a prototype. Finally, stakeholder alignment is crucial before you start. Make sure everyone involved, from team members to clients, agrees on the goals and milestones. This prevents confusion and keeps the project moving smoothly.
